name: Attempt to Delete Shadow Copies
notes: This rule triggers when a command to delete shadowcopies is executed. Malicious programs can attempt to use the Windows Management Instrumentation or Vssadmin to remove snapshots of files created by Windows.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Command (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES '(wmic|vssadmin)\.exe' AQL filter query
when the event matches LOWER("Command") MATCHES '(.*shadowcopy\s+delete.*|.*delete\s+shadows.*)' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Attempt to Delete Shadow Copies on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES '(wmic|vssadmin)\.exe' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") MATCHES '(.*shadowcopy\s+delete.*|.*delete\s+shadows.*)' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Attempt to Delete Shadow Copies
Event description: A command to delete Shadow Copies has been executed
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
